commentaries.author: Cornelius a Lapide
commentaries.content: Jesus answered, &c. He replied not to the words, for none had been spoken, but to the crafty intention of the Jews. He answered, i.e, He asked them for what cause do ye wish to stone Me? By works He means the miracles which He had wrought by the authority and supernatural aid of God the Father. And He thus quietly reproves their ingratitude and malignity. I have healed, He would say, your blind, and lame, and sick, by My Divine power, when destitute of all human aid; why do ye ungratefully repay My many kindnesses by evil treatment, and wish to stone Me?

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content: He therefore adds to this very statement, that He "had showed them many works from the Father "for none of which did He deserve to be stoned.
